target.name: Waymo
target.founder: Sebastian Thrun, Chris Urmson, Anthony Levandowski

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $126.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $183.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Waymo is a late-stage AI & Software company that has raised $16B+ and carries a last post-money valuation of $126.0B. The applicable peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms in the AI & Software sector at similar or adjacent stages, including Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, and Databricks.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Late Stage advancement, yielding $151.2B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (Anthropic at $183.0B, Together AI at $3.3B, and Labelbox at $1.0B) produces a median implied value of $3.3B, though this method displays substantial dispersion—a $182.0B spread between floor and ceiling. The extreme range in the peer set ($1.0B to $183.0B) reflects pronounced valuation variance within frontier AI.

Triangulating both methods produces a median of $77.2B, with a low bound of $2.8B and high bound of $173.9B. The current $126.0B valuation sits within this range but notably above the median, reflecting Waymo's scale, capital deployment, and market position relative to peers. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-up multiple for a company of Waymo's capital intensity and the representativeness of the peer set given extreme heterogeneity in AI sector valuations.
